channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Mandatory genetic testing in sport: World Athletics and World Boxing repeat the discredited mistakes of the past
item.description: World Athletics and World Boxing announced mandatory SRY gene testing for female category participation, echoing past discredited policies and raising significant ethical and human rights concerns in sport.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Long-term resistance training with all-cause and cause-specific mortality: assessing dose-response and joint associations with aerobic physical activity
item.description: A large prospective cohort study involving three cohorts (Health Professionals Follow-up Study, Nurses’ Health Study, Nurses’ Health Study II) found that resistance training is associated with lower all-cause and cause-specific mortality, demonstrating a dose-response relationship and joint benefits with aerobic activity.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Are There Sex Disparities in Return to Sport After Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction? A Systematic Review and Exploratory Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and exploratory meta-analysis reveals sex disparities in return-to-sport (RTS) outcomes after ACL reconstruction, with female athletes exhibiting lower RTS rates than males.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Investigation of the Effectiveness of Extracorporeal Shock Wave Therapy and Kinesiotaping in Individuals With Carpal Tunnel Syndrome: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 54 individuals with mild to moderate carpal tunnel syndrome found extracorporeal shock wave therapy and kinesiotaping, combined with splinting and exercises, to be effective.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Bone-anchored prostheses for transtibial amputation: A systematic review of outcomes, complications, and cost-effectiveness
item.description: A systematic review on bone-anchored prostheses for transtibial amputation examined outcomes, complications, and cost-effectiveness, revealing varying etiologies globally.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: SRY gene screening in elite Athletics: rationale, science and implementation of the World Athletics Regulations
item.description: World Athletics reaffirmed its policy requiring SRY gene screening for female athletes in elite competitions to ensure eligibility for the female biological sex category, outlining the rationale, science, and implementation.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Sit-to-stand test emerges as a powerful prognostic factor of future health outcomes: different versions, measurement properties and future perspectives
item.description: A commentary emphasizes the sit-to-stand test as a powerful prognostic factor for future health outcomes, correlating muscular strength with long-term health conditions and advocating for its increased use in assessment.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Randomised three-armed trial investigation of the Copenhagen Achilles tendon Rupture Treatment Algorithm (CARTA) for individualised treatment of acute Achilles tendon rupture
item.description: A multicenter, three-arm, randomized controlled trial evaluated the Copenhagen Achilles Tendon Rupture Treatment Algorithm (CARTA) for individualized treatment of acute Achilles tendon rupture, comparing it against default operative and non-operative strategies.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Long-term risk of knee replacement after ACL reconstruction using the contralateral knee as an internal control: a National Hospital Episode Statistics database study of 135 881 patients
item.description: A large population-based study (N=135,881) using national hospital data found that patients who underwent ACL reconstruction had a 4.9 times higher long-term risk of knee arthroplasty compared to their contralateral knee.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: School-based Motor Skill Interventions for Children and Adolescents with Developmental Coordination Disorder: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis investigated the effects of school-based interventions on motor performance in children and adolescents with developmental coordination disorder (DCD), finding positive results.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Using visual biofeedback to reduce step length error at fast walking speeds is feasible after stroke
item.description: A study on medRxiv suggests that using visual biofeedback to reduce step length error at fast walking speeds is feasible for individuals after stroke.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Efficacy of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation combined with soft robotic glove training for slight-to-moderate post-stroke upper limb spasticity: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: 一项随机对照试验（N=54）显示，重复经颅磁刺激（rTMS）联合软性机器人手套训练能改善轻中度卒中后上肢痉挛患者的腕屈肌痉挛和上肢运动功能。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multimodal symptom-targeted treatment for young people with persisting post-concussion symptoms: a randomised clinical trial
item.description: A single-site, open-label, assessor-blinded, parallel-group randomized clinical trial evaluated the effectiveness of "Concussion Essentials" (CE), a symptom-targeted, multidisciplinary treatment for persisting post-concussion symptoms (PPCS) in 8–18-year-olds.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Smartphone-Based Semi-Immersive Virtual Reality Enhances Upper Extremity Function in Subacute Stroke: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial (N=40) demonstrated that smartphone-based semi-immersive virtual reality augmented conventional physical therapy significantly enhanced upper extremity function in individuals with subacute stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of maintenance interventions promoting physical activity following exercise therapy for chronic conditions: an umbrella review
item.description: An umbrella review investigated the effectiveness of maintenance interventions following exercise therapy for chronic conditions on physical activity, function, fitness, and quality of life, using a narrative synthesis.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Warm-up is not a workout: a principles-based critique of continued efforts to use warm-up programmes for ACL injury prevention
item.description: A critique argues against the continued efforts to use warm-up programs for ACL injury prevention, emphasizing the distinction between warm-up and workout in terms of efficacy versus effectiveness.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Is Knee Osteoarthritis an Inevitable Consequence for Athletes after Anterior Cruciate Ligament Injury?
item.description: A perspective piece questions whether knee osteoarthritis is an inevitable consequence for athletes after anterior cruciate ligament injury, highlighting the need to consider long-term joint health.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Association between Patellofemoral Conditions and the Incidence of ACL Injury: Could Patellofemoral Conditions be a Risk Factor for ACL Injury? A 10-year Population-Based Study.
item.description: A 10-year population-based study investigates the association between patellofemoral conditions and the incidence of ACL injury, suggesting PF conditions may be a risk factor.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Accuracy of visual estimation of shoulder abduction by expert physiotherapists: a comparative study using 3D motion capture.
item.description: A comparative study found that expert physiotherapists' visual estimation of shoulder abduction, even with video assistance, showed only moderate to good agreement (ICC 0.70-0.84) with 3D motion capture, suggesting potential inaccuracies in clinical practice.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Between the Snap and the Return: A Prospective Narrative Analysis of Four Voices from inside Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction Recovery.
item.description: A prospective narrative analysis explored four patients' evolving experiences throughout anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction recovery.
item.author: PubMed
